question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user call GET /api/v1/topics/{id} to retrieve the raw bytes of a topic's adminKey via the Topic object returned by topicRepository.findById(id.getId()), and use the exposed public key material to craft a transaction that impersonates the admin signer, bypassing the requirement for admin key approval on topic updates?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user call findById() for any topic ID without any authorization check, retrieve the submitKey bytes from the returned Topic object, and use the exposed key structure to determine whether a topic requires submit-key signing before attempting unauthorized message submission?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user enumerate sequential EntityId values passed to findById() to discover topics where getSubmitKey() returns null, indicating no submit-key restriction, and then submit messages to those topics without any signer approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user enumerate sequential EntityId values passed to findById() to discover topics where getAdminKey() returns null, indicating no admin-key restriction, and then issue update or delete transactions against those topics without owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user call findById() with EntityId.EMPTY (id=0L) to probe whether topicRepository.findById(0L) returns a valid Topic object, potentially exposing key material for a system-level topic that should not be accessible without owner authorization?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any ownership or caller-identity check in findById() to read the feeScheduleKey bytes from the returned Topic, and use that key information to craft a fee schedule update transaction without the fee schedule key owner's approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user call findById() to retrieve the feeExemptKeyList from a Topic object, identify which public keys are exempt from custom fees, and then use that knowledge to forge or impersonate a fee-exempt signer to bypass custom fee payment requirements on ConsensusSubmitMessage transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findById() performs no entity-type validation before calling topicRepository.findById(id.getId()), allowing them to pass an EntityId whose getId() value corresponds to a non-topic entity, potentially retrieving key material that was not intended to be exposed through the topic API endpoint?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the EntityId bit-encoding scheme (shard<<54 | realm<<38 | num) to craft two different EntityId string representations that resolve to the same getId() long value, causing findById() to return the same Topic for both, and use this ambiguity to bypass shard/realm-scoped authorization checks on topic transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user use the orElseThrow(() -> new EntityNotFoundException('Topic not found')) behavior as a boolean oracle to enumerate which topic IDs exist in the system, and then target only existing topics with null submitKey for unauthorized message submission without signer approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user repeatedly call findById() across all valid topic IDs to harvest the adminKey bytes of every topic in the system, building a database of public keys that can be used to identify weak or reused keys and craft transactions that bypass admin-key signing requirements?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user call findById() to read the timestampRange of a Topic, determine when the adminKey or submitKey was last rotated, and use that timing information to target topics with stale or potentially compromised keys to bypass signing requirements?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of rate limiting on findById() to perform high-speed enumeration of all topic IDs, systematically identifying topics with null adminKey (immutable topics) and null submitKey (open submission topics) to target for unauthorized transactions without owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user call findById() with a crafted EntityId where getId() returns a value that collides with a topic ID in the topic table but belongs to a different shard or realm, causing the service to return key material for a topic the attacker should not be able to access, enabling unauthorized transaction authorization bypass?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the EntityId cache (Caffeine cache with 500,000 entries, 60-minute expiry) by making findById() calls that populate the cache with attacker-controlled EntityId mappings, causing subsequent legitimate calls to resolve to incorrect topic IDs and bypass proper signer verification?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user call findById() to read the submitKey from a Topic object, determine the key type (ED25519, ECDSA_SECP256K1, or threshold key) from the protobuf encoding, and use that key type information to select the most efficient attack vector for forging submit-key signatures without the owner's approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user call findById() to read the feeExemptKeyList from a Topic, identify that a specific public key is fee-exempt, and then use that knowledge to submit ConsensusSubmitMessage transactions claiming to be signed by a fee-exempt key, bypassing custom fee payment without the fee-exempt key owner's authorization?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the EntityId.of(long id) factory method's behavior where id=0 returns EntityId.EMPTY, and call findById(EntityId.EMPTY) to probe whether topicRepository.findById(0L) returns a Topic, potentially bypassing the EntityNotFoundException guard and accessing a default or system topic without authorization?
